description:
A performance-engineering script inside /home/user currently has no reliable, reproducible
    reference clock.  
    You have been asked to create a *deterministic* timestamp file that any future benchmark run
    can compare against.

    Requirements you must meet:

    1. Use **a single terminal command** – everything (directory creation, locale & timezone
       adjustment, and file write) must be done in one line.
    2. The command must:
       • run with the locale set to **en_US.UTF-8**  
       • run with the timezone forced to **UTC**  
       • leave the global system locale and timezone unchanged (i.e. only the environment of
         this command is modified).
    3. The command has to create the directory
          /home/user/profile/logs
       if it does not yet exist.
    4. It must then write one exact line to the text file
          /home/user/profile/logs/epoch_utc_time.log
       The line must be precisely:
          1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC
       (ISO-8601 date-time followed by a single space and the timezone abbreviation “UTC”, with
       no trailing or leading whitespace and a single final newline).
    5. Nothing else should be printed to the terminal.

    In summary, compose one single-line shell command that satisfies points 1-4.  
    After running it, you can verify success yourself with:

        cat /home/user/profile/logs/epoch_utc_time.log

    which should output exactly the line shown in point 4.
truth:
Expected file: /home/user/profile/logs/epoch_utc_time.log

    Expected contents (including final newline):

    1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC\n
name: task_000000_325e2f23
